title: EOG Resources, Inc. (EOG) Q4 2022 Earnings Call Transcript
text: EOG Resources, Inc. (NYSE:EOG ) Q4 2022 Results Conference Call February 24, 2023 10:00 AM ET Company Participants Tim Driggers - Chief Financial Officer Ezra Yacob - Chairman and Chief Executive Officer Billy Helms - President and Chief Operating Officer Ken Boedeker - EVP, Exploration and Production Jeff Leitzell - EVP, Exploration for Lance Terveen - SVP, Marketing David Streit - VP, Investor Relations Conference Call Participants Paul Cheng - Scotiabank Arun Jayaram - JPMorgan Doug Leggate - Bank of America Leo Mariani - MKM Partners Neal Dingmann - Truist Scott Gruber - Citigroup Jeanine Wai - Barclays Derrick Whitfield - Stifel Charles Meade - Johnson Rice Operator Good day, everyone, and welcome to EOG Resources Fourth Quarter Full Year 2022 Earnings Results Conference Call. As a reminder, the call is being recorded.

title: EOG Resources (EOG) Q4 Earnings Miss Estimates, Revenues Beat
text: Higher lease and well expenses, as well as transportation costs hurt EOG Resources' (EOG) Q4 earnings.

title: EOG Resources (EOG) Q4 Earnings Miss Estimates
text: EOG Resources (EOG) delivered earnings and revenue surprises of -0.30% and 5.07%, respectively, for the quarter ended December 2022. Do the numbers hold clues to what lies ahead for the stock?

title: EOG Resources: The Right Play Now
text: Shares of oil and gas E&P concern EOG Resources, Inc. have fallen just over 20% since reaching an all-time high on the day it reported its 3Q22 financials. Forecasted well cost inflation of 10% in FY23 and a mild winter in the U.S. and Europe have contributed to the pullback as energy prices have fallen.

title: What To Expect From EOG Stock Post Q4?

title: EOG Resources: What To Expect From Next Week's Q4 Report
text: One of the last L-48 shale producers to report, EOG will release its Q4 and full-year 2022 results next Friday before the market opens. Today I'll give investors a sneak-peak preview as to what to expect from one of the best shale operators in the business.

title: Buybacks Or Dividends? What Worked Better So Far
text: As oil and gas companies have shifted from survival to cash generation mode, their capital return strategies have come into focus. Some U.S. independents prioritize buybacks while others rely on dividends; few companies employ a mix of both strategies.

title: Technical Support in Place for Oil Stock
